behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing; missing fields return a complete missing-items error.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestrator-to-downstream handoff: behavior text says validation happens "before any downstream processing." A pure Unit test can verify field-list logic but can miss accidental downstream invocation. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess, real model output, or multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake entrypoint with missing `name` and `insurance_id`; assert error lists both fields and assert insurance/triage/scheduling modules are not called.

behaviors.description: Date-of-birth validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This is local validation logic with no module boundary requirement in the behavior text. Rejected Integration+ because no handoff/infra/model dependency is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for DOB parser/validator with cases: tomorrow, 151+ years ago, malformed strings, and valid boundary dates.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID/plan code to payer API and maps `eligible`/`not eligible`/`pending`.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets external payer API contract. Real HTTP request shape/serialization and status mapping failures can be hidden by mocked client calls. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI decision quality or multi-agentic chain is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real HTTP test server for payer API; execute insurance check and assert received payload fields (`patient_id`, `plan_code`) and returned internal state mapping for each payer status.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility becomes `pending` and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is real connectivity/unreachable behavior (timeout/connection refused), which is infrastructure-specific. In-process mocks can hide retry/timeout/socket handling bugs. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-output dependency or multi-agentic sequence in this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Point payer endpoint to an unreachable address or kill test server mid-call; assert insurance status becomes `pending` and pipeline proceeds to triage/scheduling steps.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: This checks wiring from intake config+input into model-call payload, i.e., module handoff. No requirement for model output quality. Rejected Agentic via behavior text: it only asserts payload content, not correctness of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke triage service with stub model client; capture outbound request and assert both symptom text and emergency rubric are present and correctly populated.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage marks emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath) as high urgency.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment quality for clinical symptom combinations; deterministic stubs would always pass and hide true failure. Rejected Workflow because this behavior is a single model decision, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run a fixed emergency-case evaluation set against the live triage model; assert high-urgency assignment meets defined pass threshold for emergency combinations.

behaviors.description: Triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field, not only numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser-to-storage handoff (field extraction + persistence). Unit parser tests alone may miss dropped-column/mapping issues. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about storing explanation field presence, not evaluating explanation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage response containing score+explanation through parser and persistence layer; assert explanation column/text field is stored and retrievable.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation is case-specific and includes symptom-based urgency rationale, not boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly evaluates generated explanation quality/content from live AI output; mocked responses hide this failure mode. Rejected Workflow because this is one agentic generation quality check, not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on diverse symptom cases; score explanations for symptom mention and rationale specificity using a rubric, and fail if generic boilerplate exceeds threshold.

behaviors.description: Scheduling chooses first available slot matching urgency SLA (high same-day, medium within 48h).
behaviors.justification: This is scheduling-service + calendar-data interaction and rule enforcement across modules. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess effects beyond local module/DB wiring. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Seed calendar with ordered availability and run scheduler for high/medium urgencies; assert selected slot is earliest one satisfying each urgency time window.

behaviors.description: Scheduling does not double-book when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is concurrency under shared DB behavior; mocks/in-memory fakes often miss transaction/isolation/locking races. Requires real concurrent execution against real shared storage semantics. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI-decision dependency is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Launch two concurrent intake/scheduling workers targeting same slot on real shared calendar DB; assert at most one booking succeeds and no duplicate slot row/state appears.

behaviors.description: Confirmation includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic message composition/content selection logic. Behavior text does not require external delivery infra or cross-module state race to reveal failure.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests for confirmation builder with multiple visit types; assert required fields appear and prep instructions match visit type mapping.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to orchestrated module wiring with deterministic stubbed triage, so model-quality testing is out of scope. Rejected Agentic by concrete text: "deterministic stubbed triage output." Rejected System unless implementation specifically requires real external infra for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline in test environment with stub triage and controlled insurance/scheduling adapters; assert ordered stage completion and expected final confirmation artifacts.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Pipeline includes live AI triage call; per behavior text this is a real submission through live components, so correctness depends on real model-in-the-loop execution. Rejected Workflow because only one agentic decision stage (triage) is described; other stages are non-agentic service steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end intake in staging/prod-like env with live insurance API and live triage model for a real submission fixture; assert unattended completion through confirmation and persisted records.
